methods.description: This method involves the addition of CO2 enriched air into a semi-closed circulation system of filtered natural seawater to study the effects of carbon perturbations on marine organisms.

steps.description: CO2 enriched air (0.2%) was humidified via a system of Erlenmeyer flasks and bubbled into an aerated reservoir tank (30 L), connected to incubation chambers, which contained the organisms.
steps.searchable_summary: humidify and bubble CO2 enriched air into a reservoir tank connected to incubation chambers
outputs: humidified CO2 enriched air in reservoir tank

steps.description: Gas flow rates and thereby pCO2 levels were regulated via mass flow controllers.
steps.searchable_summary: regulate gas flow rates and pCO2 levels using mass flow controllers

steps.description: The system was allowed to equilibrate for 48 hours.
steps.searchable_summary: allow system to equilibrate for 48 hours

methods.description: This method involves conducting amperometric and potentiometric microsensor measurements in a controlled environment to minimize electrical disturbance.

steps.description: All amperometric and potentiometric microsensor measurements were conducted in a Faraday cage to minimize electrical disturbance.
steps.searchable_summary: conduct microsensor measurements in a Faraday cage

steps.description: Specimens were carefully transferred with a fine brush from the incubation chambers into a flow-cell connected to the same circulation system.
steps.searchable_summary: transfer specimens to flow-cell connected to circulation system

steps.description: Net flow rates within the flow cell were adjusted volumetrically to simulate average natural in situ flow conditions.
steps.searchable_summary: adjust net flow rates in flow cell to simulate natural conditions

methods.description: This method determines the microenvironmental dynamics by positioning foraminifera and measuring O2 evolution under varying light intensities.

steps.description: Foraminifera were positioned horizontally in the middle of the flow cell resting on their central elevations.
Microsensor tips were positioned on the calcite shell surfaces of foraminifera using a stereo-microscope and a 3D-manual micromanipulator.
steps.searchable_summary: position microsensor tips on foraminifera shells

steps.description: O2 evolution within the DBL of phototrophic species was tested under varying light intensities.
steps.searchable_summary: test O2 evolution under varying light intensities

methods.description: This method involves calculating concentration differences and performing statistical analyses on the collected data.

steps.description: Hydrogen ion (H+) concentrations for dilute aqueous solutions were calculated from pH levels.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate H+ concentrations from pH levels

steps.description: Differences in concentrations between the bulk seawater and the surface of the shells were attained from the measured profiles.
Means of DO2, DH+, and DCa2+ over replicate profiles per individual were tested for normality and homogeneity of variances.
steps.searchable_summary: test for normality and homogeneity of variances

steps.description: Complete data sets of mean DO2, DH+, and DCa2+ were analyzed by Kruskal-Wallis one way analysis of variance.
steps.searchable_summary: perform Kruskal-Wallis analysis on data sets

steps.description: Group comparisons were performed using Wilcoxon signed rank test for paired samples and Wilcoxon rank sum tests for unpaired samples.
steps.searchable_summary: perform Wilcoxon tests for group comparisons

methods.description: This method describes the collection, cleaning, identification, and culturing of foraminifera specimens for experimental use.

steps.description: Specimens were hand collected from coral rubble and other substrates by SCUBA diving.
Specimens were washed off substrates, cleaned by gentle washing and sieving, and identified to species and genus level.
Samples were kept in natural seawater under low light conditions until transported to the research facility.
steps.searchable_summary: store samples in seawater under low light

steps.description: Specimens acclimatized in indoor climatic chambers for 3 weeks in natural seawater with controlled conditions.
